user_request: Prepare a detailed plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000/- advanced as a hand loan by bank transfer. There is no written agreement. The transaction is supported by bank statement and WhatsApp communications. The Defendant has failed to repay despite repeated oral demands and legal notice. Plead limitation properly. Claim interest at 12% per annum from date of loan till realization. Draft strictly in accordance with Order VII CPC. Include proper cause of action, jurisdiction, valuation, court fee and prayer clause. Use formal Indian court language suitable for filing before City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

assumptions: Jurisdiction lies in Bengaluru City Civil Court based on cause of action or defendant residence
Legal notice was duly served on the defendant
WhatsApp communications are admissible as secondary evidence under Indian Evidence Act

draft_artifacts.title: Plaint for Recovery of Money Under Order VII CPC
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE CITY CIVIL COURT, BENGALURU

CIVIL SUIT NO. {{SUIT_NUMBER}} OF {{YEAR}}

IN THE MATTER OF:
{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}}, {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}                                    ... PLAINTIFF

VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, {{DEFENDANT_AGE}}, {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}                                     ... DEFENDANT

PLAINT UNDER ORDER VII OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908

The Plaintiff above-named, in compliance with the mandatory particulars prescribed under Order VII Rule 1 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, and in conformity with the Karnataka Court-fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958, respectfully submits this Plaint, praying as follows:

1. The Plaintiff is {{PLAINTIFF_DESCRIPTION}}. The Defendant is {{DEFENDANT_DESCRIPTION}} and is personally known to the Plaintiff.

2. That on or about {{DATE_OF_LOAN}}, the Defendant approached the Plaintiff and requested an advance of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) as a hand loan for personal/business exigencies, promising to repay the same on demand or within a reasonable period.

3. That pursuant to the said request and based on an oral agreement/arrangement between the parties, and in the absence of any written agreement or promissory note, the Plaintiff on {{DATE_OF_TRANSFER}} transferred the sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) to the Defendant's bank account through electronic bank transfer, as evidenced by the bank statement annexed hereto as Annexure-A.

4. That the Defendant acknowledged receipt of the said loan amount through WhatsApp communications dated {{DATE_OF_ACKNOWLEDGMENT}}, copies of which are annexed hereto as Annexure-B, and promised to repay the same within a reasonable time.

5. That despite repeated oral demands made by the Plaintiff from {{DATE_OF_FIRST_DEMAND}} onwards, the Defendant failed and neglected to repay the said amount or any part thereof.

6. That the Plaintiff, through his Advocate, issued a legal notice dated {{DATE_OF_LEGAL_NOTICE}} calling upon the Defendant to repay the principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- along with interest at the rate of 12% per annum within 15 days from the date of receipt thereof. The said notice was duly served upon the Defendant on {{DATE_OF_SERVICE}}, as evidenced by the postal/courier receipts and acknowledgment annexed hereto as Annexure-C.

7. That the Defendant has failed, neglected and refused to repay the said principal amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- or any part thereof, or any interest thereon, despite the service of the said legal notice and despite having no lawful justification or excuse for such non-payment. The Defendant is thus liable to repay the said loan amount along with interest as claimed hereinafter.

CAUSE OF ACTION:
8. That the cause of action for the present suit first arose on {{DATE_OF_TRANSFER}} when the loan amount was advanced to the Defendant, creating an obligation upon the Defendant to repay the same. The right to sue first accrued on {{DATE_OF_ACCRUAL}} when the Defendant failed to repay the amount despite oral demands and the legal notice served upon him. The said cause of action is continuing in nature and continues to accrue de die in diem till the realization of the entire amount due and payable by the Defendant to the Plaintiff.

JURISDICTION:
9. That the Defendant resides and/or carries on business at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}, which is within the territorial jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court. Alternatively, a substantial part of the cause of action arose at Bengaluru where the loan was arranged and the bank transfer was initiated from a bank branch situated within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court. This Hon'ble Court has thus jurisdiction to entertain and try the present suit.

LIMITATION:
10. That the present suit has been instituted within the period of limitation prescribed under {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963 (Act No. 36 of 1963), which prescribes a period of three years from the date when the right to sue first accrued. The right to sue accrued on {{DATE_OF_ACCRUAL}}, and the present suit is filed well within the prescribed period of three years from the said date, and is therefore within limitation.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE:
11. That for the purpose of jurisdiction under Section 8 of the Suits Valuation Act, 1887 read with the Karnataka Court-fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958, and in compliance with Order VII Rule 1(i) and Rule 2 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, the suit is valued at Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) being the principal amount claimed. The ad valorem court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} has been paid on the said valuation as per Schedule II, Article 1 of the Karnataka Court-fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958.

RELIEF CLAIMED:
12. The Plaintiff claims the following reliefs:
(a) A decree for recovery of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) against the Defendant;
(b) Interest at the rate of 12% per annum on the said principal amount from {{DATE_OF_TRANSFER}} till the date of realization;
(c) Costs of the suit; and
(d) Any other relief or further orders that this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

PRAYER:
In view of the foregoing facts and submissions, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to:
(a) Pass a decree for recovery of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant with interest thereon at the rate of 12% per annum from {{DATE_OF_TRANSFER}} till the date of realization;
(b) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff;
(c) Pass any other order or grant any other relief as this Hon'ble Court may deem just and proper in the interest of justice.

Place: Bengaluru
Date: {{DATE_OF_FILING}}

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}
... Plaintiff

VERIFICATION:
I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify and state on solemn affirmation as follows:
1. That the contents of paragraphs 1 to 7 are true to my personal knowledge.
2. That the contents of paragraphs 8 to 12 are based on legal advice and believed to be true.
3. That the documents annexed hereto are true copies of the original documents.
4. That I have not concealed any material fact or document relevant to this case.

Solemnly affirmed at Bengaluru on this {{DATE_OF_VERIFICATION}}.

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}
... Plaintiff

LIST OF DOCUMENTS/ENCLOSURES:
1. Annexure-A: Bank statement evidencing transfer of Rs. 15,00,000/- to Defendant's account dated {{DATE_OF_TRANSFER}}.
2. Annexure-B: Printouts of WhatsApp communications between Plaintiff and Defendant acknowledging the loan dated {{DATE_OF_ACKNOWLEDGMENT}}.
3. Annexure-C: Copy of legal notice dated {{DATE_OF_LEGAL_NOTICE}} along with postal/courier receipts and acknowledgment.
4. Annexure-D: {{ADDITIONAL_DOCUMENTS}}
